text: Luke chapter 12 verses 22 through 28. People often have misunderstood or stumbled over these words. In the group, these words come from the negative form of Merrimnao. This word actually describes the effects of worry. To divide, to rip, or tear apart. And thus it has come to mean being anxious or worried so that you are divided or ripped or torn apart. Christ is saying, do not worry about your life. Don't be torn apart. Do not be full of concern about it. In 1929, J.C. Penny's business was highly unstable. He began to worry. He became sleepless. It was the beginning of the Great Depression. He worried to the extreme and contracted shingles, which is a very painful disease. He ended up having to go to the hospital, where he was given medication to tranquilize him. but it was of no help. He was just consumed with worry about his business. In fact, he felt so bad that he expected to die before morning. So he started writing farewell letters to his wife, his son, and his friends. Penny eventually recovered, but worried tore him apart. Worry involves fear of the future. You worry about what might happen to you. What if you don't have anything to eat? What if you don't have anything to drink? What if you don't have anything to wear? You look off into the future, and yet you have no control over these events. You assume. You guess. You estimate. Something bad is going to happen to you. I read a story once in Time magazine. in August 14, 1989, there was the sad story of a man from East Detroit. This man died as a result of his overwhelming fear of the future. He had taken a number of fur-trapping expeditions over the years and was bitten, often by ticks. Then he heard about Lyme disease, which is carried by deer ticks. He became obsessed with worry. He was worried he had contracted the disease. He had passed it on to his wife. The doctors tested him and assured him he didn't have Lyme disease. And if he did, it was virtually impossible to transmit it that way to his wife. But he didn't believe the doctors. He became paranoid and ended up killing himself, and his wife. The police found the man's mailbox jammed with material describing Lyme disease. In a brief note, confirming a doctor's appointment for another Lyme disease test. Worry overwhelmed the man. Still, you think about all the possibilities, all the probabilities. You try to weigh at all and discern what is likely to happen to you. And yet because you can't be sure and you can't control the future, it begins to tear you apart inwardly. There is fretting. There is the nail biting. A churning stomach. Alcers and so forth. And you have perpetual uneasiness about what might happen to you. Worry is actually fear then of the future. Let me say that worry distracts you from your main purpose in life, which is to honor God. You can become so absorbed. with the bad things which may happen to you in the future, that you fail to do your present duty. In other words, worry paralyzes you in a way. Martha had a large feast at her home for Jesus and his disciples. She wanted to be the perfect hostess. Everything had to be just right. She worked herself up into a frenzy about this dinner. She worried about everything. You can imagine what it would be like to have a large feast at your home. Her worrying caused her to lose sight of what was truly important, as Jesus said, but one thing is needful, and Mary hath chosen that good part which shall not be taken away from her. There's a danger, then, of being distracted from your main purpose in life by anxiety over earthly things. So you must be careful. You don't want to be like Martha. So what is worry? We talked about the meaning of take no thought, that worrying is actually fear of the future, and worrying distracts you from your main purpose in life. But let me say also this morning that Christ is not teaching you to totally disregard your needs. many misguided people have taken the words, take no thought, very literally, and decided to simply live by faith, or at least that's what they call it, which to them means, take no pains to provide for any future needs. You're not to worry because God is in control of your life. Notice verse 23, where Christ says, the life is more than meat and the body more than Raymond. Your life is a gift from God. How did you get it? Where did it come from? Your life is more valuable than meat, and your body more valuable than clothing. Remember, you are God's creation. Your life is a gift from God. When you think about all the animals, as well as all the human beings on this earth, he provides food for us. Doesn't he?
